symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid evolution of a pigmented lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Evolution ('E') is the strongest historical melanoma warning sign.
Dysplastic naevi are relatively stable, not rapidly evolving.
Asymmetry, border/colour irregularity, diameter >6mm and an outlier lesion strongly predict melanoma.
SKs are 'stuck-on' waxy without true variegation/irregular evolution.

investigation_details.expected_result: Determines melanoma diagnosis, Breslow thickness, ulceration and staging
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without excision the diagnosis and stage remain unknown and treatment is delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only excisional histology can confirm/exclude melanoma and stage it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent complete excisional biopsy (2mm margin) for histopathology/Breslow thickness
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive diagnosis and staging of a suspected melanoma.
Assess for other suspicious lesions and regional nodes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Shave/partial biopsy of the suspected melanoma
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Partial/shave biopsy can compromise Breslow staging — avoid for suspected melanoma.
